AAPL.rationale: P/E has re-expanded to 32.4x on flat TTM EPS ($6.14) driven entirely by the Apple Intelligence narrative, recreating the same overvaluation pattern I was bearish on in Jan 2024 — the catalyst is real but speculative and already priced in.
key_factors: P/E re-expanded from 26.5x (Mar 2024) to 32.4x (Jun 28) on zero EPS growth — pure multiple expansion driven by AI narrative
TTM EPS ~$6.14 essentially flat vs FY2023 $6.13 despite 7% revenue growth; margin improvement (46.6% gross) is real but incremental
Stock surged 23% in 3 weeks post-WWDC Apple Intelligence announcement, with analysts mixed on whether features drive near-term upgrade cycle
Prior Apr 2024 bullish call (+0.25) was based on P/E derating to 26.5x — that thesis is now fully invalidated by the re-rating

Stock at $333 is down 12% from its $379 April peak but still +35% YoY, with normalized earnings power of $14-16 implying true P/E of 21-24x on current price
Revenue essentially flat YoY in Q1 2024 ($15.8B vs $15.9B) while operating income grew 29% — margin expansion driven by mix/cost cuts, not top-line growth
Analyst consensus PT of $379 implies 14% upside, but at cycle-peak profitability the risk/reward is asymmetric to the downside if margins revert

trace.final_explanation: openclaw_per_ticker: 30/30 tickers produced a view via openclaw.

AMGN.final_explanation: AMGN's obesity-pipeline-driven re-rating has pushed the stock to 44.5x TTM PE with compressed margins and $64B debt, while the MariTide Phase 2 catalyst remains 6+ months away — risk/reward skews negative.

AMZN.final_explanation: AMZN's operating margin inflection is the dominant driver — earnings are growing dramatically faster than the stock price, and the margin expansion runway remains long as logistics efficiency and AWS recovery continue.

BA.final_explanation: Boeing's fundamental deterioration has accelerated since prior assessments — the 737 MAX crisis, FAA production cap, and leadership exodus create a turnaround timeline that extends well beyond a one-year horizon, making the risk-reward unfavorable despite the stock's 31% decline.

DIS.final_explanation: DIS turnaround is gaining real traction — streaming profitable, box office surging, governance resolved — and the 19% pullback from $122 to $99 creates a more balanced risk/reward, though the 62.6x P/E still demands execution.

HD.final_explanation: HD's bearish thesis from prior decisions is partially playing out (stock down ~10% from April), but the stock still demands a premium valuation without fundamental improvement — comps remain negative, margins are compressed, and the housing cycle has not turned. Mild negative lean maintained.

INTC.final_explanation: INTC's fundamental deterioration has been largely priced in after a 38% decline from highs; at 1.27x book value the stock offers limited further downside but lacks a clear recovery catalyst within a year.

JNJ.final_explanation: JNJ's valuation discount has widened since April despite solid fundamentals, while talc litigation is moving toward a potential resolution — the risk-reward has improved.

KO.final_explanation: KO: quality defensive compounder but valuation has re-rated to 25.5x P/E near highs with decelerating growth — mildly positive, limited edge.

MRK.final_explanation: MRK remains a quality compounder with strong Q1 execution and pipeline diversification, but the re-rating to ~25x normalized PE from ~15x a year ago means much of the good news is priced in; the 7% pullback modestly improves risk/reward but isn't enough to make it clearly attractive given patent cliff and competitive headwinds.

MSFT.final_explanation: MSFT quality is undeniable but the multiple has expanded to 39x P/E while growth is plateauing — risk-adjusted reward is mildly negative at this price.

NKE.final_explanation: NKE remains in fundamental deterioration with an elongated revenue trough; the 20% post-earnings crash and P/E compression from 33x to ~20x partially price in the risk, but declining EPS and lack of near-term catalysts keep risk-reward unattractive.

VZ.final_explanation: VZ remains mildly attractive: forward P/E ~9.5x and 6.5% dividend yield offer income support, but the stock has already rallied 35% from its Oct 2023 lows and revenue continues declining ~2% YoY, limiting further re-rating potential.
